rag_rules: Preserve AADL architecture by inserting annex blocks in valid component or implementation scopes
Use component type annexes for assumptions, guarantees, constants, and equations
Use implementation annexes for implementation-level bindings such as assignments
Place output dependency guarantees in component type scopes using visible outputs and inputs
Use assign only for implementation contracts when the left-hand side is an existing output feature or declared auxiliary variable
generic_rules: Maintain scope consistency between AADL architecture and AGREE annex blocks
Use valid AGREE scalar types (int, real, bool) in declarations
Avoid range comparisons or 0/1 equality over Boolean, enumeration-like, or opaque data ports
Align helper declaration types with the expression types in comparisons
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use initialized temporal recurrence for stateful equations
Use nested if ... then ... else for branching logic
Use AGREE logical operators (and, or, not, =>) in contracts
anti_patterns: Do not place guarantee statements in implementation scope
Do not use implementation-only subcomponent paths in component type annexes
Do not convert Boolean domain values into integers in comparisons
irrelevant_topics: AGREE, annex, assume, guarantee, assign, validator, or repair
